summary: The PoC demonstrates that scanning a URL with an unbounded HTTP response causes the picklescan process to consume excessive memory and crash with MemoryError or be killed by the OS OOM killer.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the picklescan scanner to attempt loading a large HTTP response into memory, resulting in memory exhaustion and process termination.
actual_observed_result: The PoC successfully triggered memory exhaustion. The scanner process was killed by the OS after attempting to load the large response body.
verification_method: The run.sh script monitors the scanner process memory usage and verifies it exceeds reasonable limits (e.g., > 1GB) before being killed, confirming the DoS condition.
evidence_produced_by_run: Process was terminated by SIGKILL (OOM killer) or crashed with MemoryError
Scanner did not complete scanning the file
System memory monitoring showed exhaustion event

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
